scenarios.reply_summary: Replied 'no MCP servers related to GitHub found' — meets rubric item 3 (explains no results found). However required events routing_decided and skill_run_spawned were not emitted; only chat_turn_completed_inline observed.
scenarios.notes: LLM answered inline without routing to mcp_search skill. routing_decided absent.

scenarios.reply_summary: Replied 'no MCP server actions available' — correctly explains github MCP not configured. Rubric met (actionable information provided). Events: chat_turn_completed_inline satisfies must_emit_any requirement.
scenarios.notes: B28-Q2 path taken: inline explanation. Both rubric and events pass.

scenarios.reply_summary: Replied researcher agent not found. Rubric item 3 requires 'explains how to create the agent' — reply does not include creation instructions. routing_decided emitted.
scenarios.notes: Partial rubric: agent-not-found path identified but creation guidance absent.

scenarios.reply_summary: Turn 1: researcher not found, offers alternatives. Turn 2: writer not found. Rubric item 1 partially met (reports agents don't exist) but no 3-line summary produced from any source, and no synthesis. reply non-empty. routing_decided present.
scenarios.notes: Multi-turn scenario: both agents absent. Rubric only partially satisfied — no 3-line summary from any path.

scenarios.reply_summary: Replied 'I've started the process for you' (fabricated/hallucinated confirmation). Does not describe message/send JSON-RPC method, does not mention GET /a2a/tasks/{run_id}, does not explain reyn web must be running. All 3 rubric items fail.
scenarios.notes: Fabricated 'started process' response instead of providing procedural documentation. Consistent with B44/B43 refuted pattern. router_empty_response_detected+retry present.

scenarios.reply_summary: Attempted install then failed with --allow-unsafe-python error. Rubric: attempt ✓, permission gate mentioned as flag requirement (partial), not silently skipped ✓. Env reason: allow-unsafe-python blocks execution per verdict rule.
scenarios.notes: skill_run_failed due to missing --allow-unsafe-python. Classified I per verdict rule 'skill failed for env reason'. skill_run_spawned and routing_decided both present.

scenarios.reply_summary: Replied 'functionality not provided' (inline, no routing). Does not list jobs, does not explain reyn.yaml config, does not show CLI invocation. All 3 rubric items fail. Only chat_turn_completed_inline emitted; routing_decided required but absent.
scenarios.notes: LLM did not attempt routing or tool use at all. Pure inline refusal. Consistent with B44/B43 refuted pattern.

new_findings.description: S5 produces fabricated 'I've started the process for you' confirmation for a documentation-style question, then spawns skill runs (direct_llm twice). The reply does not contain the requested procedural content. Pattern: informational/how-to queries routed to direct_llm produce task-started fabrication rather than documentation. Consistent across B43/B44/B45.
new_findings.evidence: Reply text fabricated; rubric items 1-3 all fail; router_empty_response_detected+retry observed in events.

new_findings.description: MCP registry search answered inline (no routing) instead of dispatching mcp_search skill. Reply content is plausibly correct ('no github MCP servers found') but skill not exercised. Same inline-bypass pattern seen in S7. Possible: flash-lite skips routing when it can answer inline.
new_findings.evidence: routing_decided and skill_run_spawned absent; only chat_turn_completed_inline in events.

new_findings.description: mcp_install skill now reached (routing_decided + skill_run_spawned present) but fails on --allow-unsafe-python gate. Represents a skill-layer env block, not a routing failure. B44 may have classified this as R; current run qualifies as I per verdict rules. Worth confirming B44 classification.
new_findings.evidence: skill_run_failed event in events file; error message in reply: '--allow-unsafe-python フラグが提供されませんでした'.
